heading: Regular Marine Corps: judge advocates
label: 8138 Regular Marine Corps: judge advocates
text: With the approval of the Secretary of the Navy, any regular officer on the active-duty list of the Marine Corps who is qualified under may, upon his application, be designated as a judge advocate.section 827(b) of this title
notes: Amendments2018— renumbered as this section.Pub. L. 115–232section 5587a of this title1980— struck out designation “(a)” before “With the approval of the Secretary”, substituted “active-duty list” for “active list”, and struck out subsec. (b) which provided that, for the purposes of determining lineal position, permanent grade, seniority in permanent grade, and eligibility for promotion, a person appointed to the active list of the Marine Corps with a view to designation as a judge advocate could be credited with the amount of service prescribed by the Secretary of the Navy, but not more than three years.Pub. L. 96–513

Effective Date of 2018 AmendmentAmendment by effective , with provision for the coordination of amendments and special rule for certain redesignations, see , set out as a note preceding .Pub. L. 115–232section 800 of Pub. L. 115–232section 3001 of this titleFeb. 1, 2019
